svg.title: PLC 2 Breadboard Layout - Vertical Breadboard
svg.text: PLC 2 Breadboard: Arduino Uno R3 + Sensors + MAX485
Vertical breadboard, 64 rows, power rails both sides, split at e/f

g.text: 2. Arduino GND (left, 5th pin) to breadboard - rail
Module sits OFF the breadboard. Use 8 female-to-male
jumper wires from module pins to breadboard:
B to row 1, col a (twisted pair out from col e)
A to row 2, col a (twisted pair out from col e)
RO to row 4, col a (jumper col e to Arduino D0)
DI to row 5, col a (jumper col e to Arduino D1)
DE to row 6, col a } same row = tied
RE to row 6, col b } jumper col e to Arduino D2
Push in. VCC row to + rail, GND row to - rail, S row to A0
A0 is on the LEFT header, top of the ANALOG IN section
VCC(+) to + rail, GND(-) to - rail, S to D4 (right header)
D4 is on the RIGHT header, between pins 5 and 3
VCC to + rail, GND to - rail, OUT to A2
VCC to + rail, GND to - rail
TRIG to D7 (right header), ECHO to D6 (right header)
VCC to + rail, GND to - rail, OUT to D3 (right header)
1. Pull jumper wires from D0 and D1
3. Reconnect jumpers to D0 and D1
Across A and B at each end of RS-485 bus (if cable over 30cm)
Rows 31-64 are free for expansion or debugging.
Right half of breadboard (f-j) entirely unused.
